reasoning: The '0.000' reference value is an automated fetching artifact from before data was populated, and the question resolves based on the actual average. Time-series data of conflict events like 'Battles' is typically right-skewed, meaning the mean is pulled up by a few high-intensity months, so a randomly selected month has a <50% chance of exceeding the 12-month mean (historically ~40% base rate on such forecasting questions). Additionally, the recent presidential elections in October 2025 likely caused a significant spike in violence. Because the 360-day average includes this election peak, the threshold will be unusually elevated. Consequently, post-election months are more likely to fall below this elevated average as violence reverts to a lower baseline. The November 30 window captures the immediate post-election phase but is expected to cool down compared to October. The February 21 window (Jan 22-Feb 21) captures 'Youth Day' on February 11, which Anglophone separatists historically target for disruption, providing a seasonal boost to conflict probability. Following the instructions, the probabilities also converge toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon increases.
